serviceHero.headline: Homeowner Insurance
serviceHero.subline: Protect your most valuable asset. We compare leading carriers to find the best rate on comprehensive home coverage.

whatItCovers.headline: What Homeowner Insurance Covers

included.title: Dwelling Coverage
included.description: Rebuilds or repairs your home's structure if damaged by covered perils like fire, windstorm, or vandalism.

included.title: Personal Property
included.description: Replaces your belongings — furniture, electronics, clothing — if stolen or damaged.

included.title: Liability Protection
included.description: Covers legal expenses and medical bills if someone is injured on your property.

included.title: Additional Living Expenses
included.description: Pays for temporary housing and extra costs if your home is uninhabitable after a covered loss.

included.title: Other Structures
included.description: Covers detached garages, sheds, fences, and other structures on your property.

excluded.title: Flood Damage
excluded.description: Standard homeowner policies do not cover flooding. Separate flood insurance is available through NFIP or private carriers.

excluded.title: Earthquake
excluded.description: Earthquake coverage requires a separate policy or endorsement.

excluded.title: Normal Wear & Tear
excluded.description: Gradual deterioration, mold, and maintenance issues are not covered.

whatItCovers.whoNeedsIt: If you own a home, condo, or co-op, you need homeowner or dwelling insurance. Most mortgage lenders require it as a condition of the loan. Even if your home is paid off, the risk of fire, storm damage, or liability without coverage is significant.

whyUs.headline: Why Get Home Insurance Through Peerless?

points.title: 30+ Carrier Options

points.title: Claims Advocacy

quoteProcess.headline: How to Get a Home Insurance Quote

steps.title: Property Details
steps.description: Address, year built, square footage, and construction type.

steps.title: Current Coverage
steps.description: Who you're currently insured with and what you're paying.

steps.title: Coverage Preferences
steps.description: Dwelling replacement cost, desired deductible, and any valuable items to schedule separately.

rateFactors.headline: What Affects Your Home Insurance Rate

factors.description: Older homes with outdated systems (electrical, plumbing, roof) cost more to insure.
Proximity to fire stations, flood zones, and high-crime areas affects your premium.
Insuring for full replacement cost vs. actual cash value significantly affects your premium.
Higher deductibles lower your premium but increase out-of-pocket costs after a claim.
A history of prior claims can increase your rate or limit carrier options.
Smoke detectors, alarm systems, and deadbolt locks may qualify for discounts.

tips: Bundle with your auto policy for the biggest single discount
Install a monitored security system for 5–15% savings
Update your roof — carriers offer better rates for newer roofs
Review your coverage annually at renewal to ensure it keeps pace with home value changes

items.question: How much home insurance do I need?
items.answer: Your dwelling coverage should equal the full replacement cost of your home — what it would cost to rebuild from scratch, not the market value. We'll calculate this for you.

items.question: Does homeowner insurance cover flooding?
items.answer: No. Standard homeowner policies exclude flood damage. If you're in a flood zone, separate flood insurance through NFIP or private carriers is essential.

items.question: Will my premium go up after I file a claim?
items.answer: Filing a claim can increase your premium, particularly for at-fault or water damage claims. We advise clients on when it makes sense to file vs. pay out of pocket.

items.question: What is an umbrella policy and do I need one?
items.answer: An umbrella policy provides additional liability coverage above your home and auto limits. It's recommended if you have significant assets to protect.

items.question: How often should I review my homeowner coverage?
items.answer: We recommend annual reviews, especially after renovations, major purchases, or when home values change significantly in your area.
